config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan সমাজ, karena itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas untuk menindas minoritas. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati hak dasar tiap orang. Bagi saya, prioritas yang sehat adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become a license for abuse. From my perspective in China, I understand the appeal of social stability and collective goals, since people often value harmony, order, and shared responsibility, but those goals are not legitimate if they erase basic personal rights. A strong society should protect individuals first enough that people can live with dignity, then ask them to contribute to the common good. So I would say the relationship should be balanced, with society having priority only in limited cases like public safety, not as a general rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil atau yang berbeda pandangan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi menurut saya justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi hak dasar orang per orang, lalu baru meminta kontribusi pada kepentingan publik. Jadi, prioritas itu harus bersyarat dan sangat terbatas, bukan prinsip mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests a general priority over individual rights, because that easily turns into a blank check for coercion. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, but I also know that “for the greater good” can be used to silence dissent or push unfair burdens onto ordinary people. A healthy society should protect basic rights first, then ask individuals to contribute to the common good in ways that are lawful and reasonable. So I would only accept society taking priority in narrow cases like genuine public safety or emergencies, not as a broad principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup-nutupi keputusan yang sebenarnya merugikan warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang kritis. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak dasar setiap orang dulu, lalu membatasi hak itu hanya bila benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan jelas dasar hukumnya. Jadi, prioritas masyarakat boleh ada dalam kondisi darurat tertentu, tetapi bukan sebagai prinsip utama yang mengalahkan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the claim as a general rule, because in my view it is too easy for “society’s interests” to become an excuse for arbitrary power. Coming from China, I do understand why people value stability, harmony, and collective responsibility, but I also think those values are only legitimate when basic individual rights are protected first. If the rights of ordinary people can be overridden whenever someone says it is for the common good, then the common good is no longer trustworthy. So I would limit society’s priority to narrow, clearly defined emergencies, not make it the default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
